# Objective 11.5 — Schema Designer UI

**Phase:** 0 (Foundation) · **Module:** Data Management Module
**Status:** Ready for development
**Depends on:** Obj 11 (Data Management Foundation & Schema Designer — backend), Obj 06 (RBAC), Obj 03 (Observability)
**Blocks:** Obj 18 (Data Browser & Editor — shares Monaco/diagram primitives), Obj 31 (Auto-Generated Documentation — references designer)

---

## Why this exists

Objective 11 originally bundled the schema-designer **service layer** (validators, migration planner, versioning, templates, isolation) with the schema-designer **UI** (Diagram, Table, Code views; capability-aware controls; deploy/rollback flows). Verification on 2026-05-03 found the backend complete and exhaustively tested while the web app at `apps/web/src/` was effectively empty (`index.ts` + `status.ts` only).

Rather than block Stage One progress on a UI that has no dependants until Objective 18, the UI is split into this follow-on objective. Objective 11 is closed against its backend Definition of Done; the user-facing surface lands here.

This split is honest, not aspirational. Obj 11 backend services (`SchemaService`, `SchemaValidator`, `MigrationPlanner`, isolation tests, templates, audit events, ADRs 0094–0098) are proven via tests and the auto-generated REST API in Obj 12. The UI consumes the same service layer through the API.

---

## Scope

### In Scope

- The `apps/web/src/data-management/schema-designer/` UI module
- **Three synchronised views** of one schema model:
  - **Diagram view** — `@xyflow/react`-based relationship graph; drag to create relations; pan/zoom; auto-layout
  - **Table view** — spreadsheet-style table & column editor; keyboard-first
  - **Code view** — Monaco editor on the canonical JSON/YAML schema representation
- **Capability-aware controls** — per ADR-0097, controls disabled or annotated when the active database driver doesn't support the feature (e.g. arrays disabled on MSSQL, FK shown as advisory on Mongo)
- **Lifecycle flows**:
  - Create from blank or template (5 templates from Obj 11 backend: blank, blog, CRM, task tracker, ecommerce)
  - Edit → validate → preview migration → apply
  - Concurrent-edit conflict resolution UX (optimistic locking; show diff, manual merge)
  - Rollback to prior version (version history view)
  - Export schema JSON; import schema JSON
  - Deploy approval flow (routes through `ApprovalRoutingEngine` from Obj 6)
- **PII tagging UI** — column-level toggles for `isPii` + `piiCategory`, integrating with the personal data registry from Obj 7
- **API explorer page** at `/data-management/<schema>/api-explorer` — wraps the OpenAPI 3.1 spec from Obj 12 in a Swagger UI / Redoc component (carved out of Obj 12 by the same logic)
- **Storybook stories** for every component
- **Accessibility** — axe-core in CI; keyboard-only flows verified; screen-reader labels on all controls
- **Light + dark themes** — using design tokens, no hardcoded colours

### Out of Scope (Belongs to Later Objectives)

- Data browser / row editor (Obj 18)
- Query console (Obj 17)
- AI-assisted schema generation (deferred; AI Pipeline integration)
- Multi-user real-time collaboration (deferred; out of scope for v1 — optimistic locking with conflict UX is sufficient)
- Schema diff against an external database connection (deferred)

---

## Locked Decisions

| Decision                | Choice                                                                                                  | Rationale                                                    |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |
| UI framework            | React 18 + shadcn/ui (established Obj 1)                                                                | Consistency with the rest of the web app                     |
| Diagram library         | `@xyflow/react`                                                                                         | Locked in Obj 11; standard, maintained                       |
| Code editor             | Monaco                                                                                                  | Industry standard; language-server support; Obj 17 reuses it |
| Schema canonical format | JSON (the same shape `SchemaService` exposes); YAML view is a presentation transform                    | One source of truth                                          |
| State management        | Zustand for designer state; React Query for server state                                                | Lightweight; matches existing app patterns                   |
| Form library            | React Hook Form + zod resolver (schemas reused from `SchemaService`)                                    | Zero schema drift between client and server validation       |
| Routing                 | Next.js App Router                                                                                      | Matches the rest of `apps/web`                               |
| Approval UX             | Inline banner when policy demands review; deploy disabled until approved                                | Reuse Obj 6 routing engine                                   |
| Conflict resolution UX  | Three-way diff view: their version / your version / merge result; manual confirm to apply merged result | Optimistic locking; no silent overwrite                      |
| Capability indication   | Disabled control + tooltip explaining the database limitation; link to capability matrix                | Per ADR-0097                                                 |
| Telemetry               | Every designer action emits a metric; trace spans for save/validate/preview/apply                       | Per Obj 3                                                    |

---

## Definition of Done

**Module skeleton**

- [ ] `apps/web/src/data-management/schema-designer/` exists with subdirectories for components, hooks, state, views
- [ ] Routing: `/data-management`, `/data-management/<schema>`, `/data-management/<schema>/api-explorer`, `/data-management/<schema>/history`
- [ ] Layout shell with workspace switcher, schema list nav, view tabs (Diagram / Table / Code)

**Three views**

- [ ] Diagram view renders, supports pan/zoom, supports drag-create relations, auto-layout works
- [ ] Table view edits all column properties; keyboard navigation works
- [ ] Code view edits canonical JSON; Monaco shows zod-derived JSON Schema validation
- [ ] All three views read & write the same in-memory schema model; switching views preserves edits

**Lifecycle flows**

- [ ] Create from blank works
- [ ] Create from each of 5 templates works
- [ ] Edit → validate (capability-aware) → preview migration (calls `SchemaService.previewMigration`) → apply
- [ ] Approval routing: deploy is gated when policy requires it; reviewer sees diff; on approval, deploy proceeds
- [ ] Rollback to prior version works; history view lists all versions with author and timestamp
- [ ] Export downloads JSON; import accepts JSON; round-trip is byte-equivalent
- [ ] Concurrent edit detection: simulating two clients editing the same schema produces a conflict UX, not a silent overwrite

**Capability-aware UX**

- [ ] Postgres workspace: all features enabled
- [ ] MSSQL workspace: array columns disabled with tooltip; computed columns flagged per capability matrix
- [ ] Mongo workspace: foreign keys shown as advisory with banner; check-constraint UI hidden
- [ ] Capability differences linked from each disabled control to `docs/architecture/capability-matrix.md`

**PII**

- [ ] Per-column `isPii` toggle and `piiCategory` selector; saving updates `SchemaService` state
- [ ] Heuristic suggestions (from `schema-model.ts:23-71`) surface as "did you mean to mark this PII?" hints

**API Explorer**

- [ ] `/api-explorer` page loads the live OpenAPI 3.1 spec from `GET /api/v1/data/<workspace>/openapi.json`
- [ ] Try-it-now works for authenticated users using their session token
- [ ] Sample request snippets for curl / JavaScript / Python

**Quality**

- [ ] Storybook story for every component (>= 1 per file, with relevant variants)
- [ ] axe-core passes in CI; zero violations on every page
- [ ] Keyboard-only walkthrough of the create-edit-deploy flow recorded and passes
- [ ] Light theme & dark theme both pass visual review
- [ ] Bundle size budget: schema-designer route group under 350 KB gzipped (Monaco lazy-loaded)
- [ ] Lighthouse CI: performance ≥ 80, accessibility = 100

**Telemetry**

- [ ] Every designer action emits `platform_schema_designer_actions_total{action,workspace}`
- [ ] Save/validate/preview/apply each have a trace span
- [ ] Errors reported to GlitchTip with workspace + correlation ID

**Documentation**

- [ ] `docs/user-guides/schema-designer.md` — written for end users (workspace admins)
- [ ] `docs/architecture/schema-designer-ui.md` — written for contributors (component map, state model, view-sync invariants)
- [ ] ADRs (see below)

---

## ADRs to Write

- **ADR-0119: Three Synchronised Views over One Schema Model** — why three views, how sync is enforced, why not a single primary view
- **ADR-0120: Optimistic Locking with Manual Merge UX** — why no real-time collaboration in v1; what the conflict experience is
- **ADR-0121: Monaco for Schema Code View** — why Monaco over CodeMirror; lazy-loading strategy; bundle implications

_(Numbers adjusted from original 0105–0107 to 0119–0121 to avoid conflict with Objective 13's GraphQL ADRs which were written first.)_

(ADR-0097 capability-aware UX already exists from Obj 11 and applies; no re-litigation.)

---

## Verification Steps

1. Create a new schema from the blank template, add 3 tables with relationships in the Diagram view, switch to Table view — relationships and columns are visible and editable.
2. Switch to Code view — JSON matches the visual model; edit a column type in JSON and switch back to Table view; the change is reflected.
3. Save the schema; reload the page; the schema is restored exactly.
4. Create a new schema from the CRM template; verify the seeded tables and columns match the template.
5. On a Postgres workspace, add an `text[]` column — accepted. On a MSSQL workspace, the array option is disabled with tooltip referencing capability matrix.
6. On a Mongo workspace, add a foreign key — banner explains "advisory only on Mongo"; deploy proceeds with the relation persisted as an FK metadata flag.
7. Edit a deployed schema; click Preview Migration — modal shows step-by-step migration plan from `SchemaService.previewMigration`.
8. Apply the migration; verify the target database reflects the change; verify a new schema version is recorded.
9. From history view, roll back to the previous version; the database state matches the prior version.
10. Two browser tabs open the same schema; tab A edits column X; tab B edits column Y; tab A saves; tab B attempts save — conflict UX appears, three-way diff renders, manual merge resolves cleanly.
11. Mark a column as PII with category `email`; verify the personal data registry from Obj 7 lists the column under that category.
12. Open `/api-explorer`; verify the OpenAPI document loads and try-it-now succeeds for a list endpoint.
13. axe-core run on every designer page returns zero violations.
14. Keyboard-only walkthrough: tab through create → edit → validate → preview → apply; every step reachable without mouse.
15. Lighthouse CI run on the schema designer route: performance ≥ 80, accessibility = 100.
16. Run Storybook in CI; every story renders without console errors.
17. Toggle light/dark theme on every page; no hardcoded colours visible.
18. Approval routing: as a non-architect, attempt to deploy a schema where policy requires architect approval — deploy is blocked with explanatory banner; an architect approves; deploy proceeds.
19. Export a schema; delete it; import the exported JSON; the schema is restored byte-equivalent (compared via `JSON.stringify` after canonical sort).
20. Bundle analyser confirms the schema-designer route group is under 350 KB gzipped with Monaco code-split.

If all 20 pass, the objective is met.

---

## Anti-Patterns to Refuse

- **Building a fourth view "for completeness."** Three views are deliberate; each maps to a real user mode (visual exploration, fast editing, exact control). A fourth dilutes mental model.
- **Real-time multi-user collaboration in v1.** Out of scope; optimistic locking + conflict UX is the v1 contract.
- **Bypassing the service layer for "speed."** All state changes go through `SchemaService` via the API. No direct database edits from the UI.
- **Hardcoded colours, fonts, or spacing.** Design tokens only.
- **Shipping without Storybook stories.** Stories are documentation, regression evidence, and review surface in one.
- **Suppressing axe-core failures.** Accessibility violations are bugs, not style issues.
- **Custom drag-drop implementation when @xyflow/react covers it.** Reuse the locked dependency.
- **Inline-editing the canonical JSON in a textarea.** Monaco only, with proper schema validation.

---

## What Comes Next

With Obj 11.5 complete, the Data Management Module has a fully usable schema designer plus a live API explorer that wraps Obj 12's auto-generated REST API. The next user-facing surface is **Objective 18: Data Browser & Editor**, which reuses the Monaco primitive and the capability-aware control patterns established here.

The module's user-visible feature progression is:

- 11 (backend) → 11.5 (designer UI) → 12 (REST API + explorer surfaces here) → 18 (data browser) → 17 (query console)

Each layer reuses primitives from the prior. Obj 11.5 is the first place those primitives are introduced.
